default_creds.context: MinIO object store (raw ingested files) — MINIO_ACCESS_KEY/MINIO_SECRET_KEY in docker-compose-linux.yaml; exposed on host port 9001 (console) / 9000 in v1
MySQL metadata DB — MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=123456 in docker-compose-linux.yaml; host port 3306 (v1) / 3316 (v2)
Elasticsearch — xpack.security.enabled=false; fully open on host port 9200 (v1) / 9210 (v2)

install_tell: git clone https://github.com/netease-youdao/QAnything.git && cd QAnything && bash run.sh   (or: docker compose -f docker-compose-linux.yaml up)
misconfig_patterns: Port 8777 exposed to the internet with the API answering POST /api/local_doc_qa/* for any free-text user_id (no tenancy isolation — user_id is client-supplied, so guessing/iterating user_id enumerates other tenants' KBs)
Host-networking mode (v2) leaking Elasticsearch 9200, Milvus 19530, MySQL 3306, MinIO 9000/9001 directly onto the public interface
Default backing-store creds left unchanged (minioadmin/minioadmin, root/123456, ES security off)
LLM API key stored in front-end settings on an unauth instance -> key theft + LLM-bill abuse (llmjacking)

response_markers: JSON response with top-level "code":200 and "data" array of knowledge-base objects (kb_id, kb_name)
POST body {"user_id":"zzp"} returns 200 with KB list and no auth challenge
active_probe.false_positive_check: Do not match on generic strings like "QA", "chat", or "Anything" — they collide with thousands of unrelated apps. Anchor on the co-occurrence of (a) the literal path segment `/api/local_doc_qa/` AND (b) port 8777 serving a frontend at `/qanything/` with `<title>QAnything</title>`. The decisive non-FP confirmation is a POST to `/api/local_doc_qa/list_knowledge_base` with body `{"user_id":"zzp"}` returning a JSON envelope `{"code":200,"msg":"success","data":[...]}` — the `local_doc_qa` route prefix and the JSON code/msg/data envelope are vendor-unique. A bare 200 on `/` or a reachable 8777 banner alone is insufficient (could be any host-networked container); require the local_doc_qa JSON envelope. Watch for honeypots echoing the path without the structured KB-object schema.

deployment_tells: Frontend served at path http://HOST:8777/qanything/ (vendor-unique URL prefix)
Backend REST under /api/local_doc_qa/ on the same 8777 port

Companion ports from the compose stack commonly co-exposed: Elasticsearch (9200/9210), Milvus standalone (19530/19540), MySQL (3306/3316), MinIO (9000-9001) — a host showing 8777 + 19530 + 9200 together is a strong QAnything stack signature
v1 uses bridge port-map 8777:8777; v2 qanything_local runs network_mode: host (so all internal service ports can leak onto the host if firewalling is absent)
Self-hosted Docker-Compose, NetEase Youdao embedding+rerank stack (BCEmbedding) bundled — distinguishes it from library-style RAG
pivot_paths: POST /api/local_doc_qa/list_files -> inventory ingested documents per KB (names/status only, restraint-compliant)
Iterate the client-supplied user_id to test cross-tenant KB enumeration (names are the finding; do not exfiltrate file bodies)
POST /api/local_doc_qa/get_bot_info -> read bot/agent config including model + base_url (confirm whether an upstream LLM key is configured; do not extract the secret)
Probe co-exposed Elasticsearch 9200 /_cat/indices and Milvus 19530 for unauth schema (metadata only)
Check MinIO console 9001 for minioadmin default (login page reachability only — do not authenticate)
vulnerabilities: CVE-2024-25722 (GHSA-68p8-j476-8w35) CRITICAL CVSS 9.8 — SQL injection in qanything_kernel/connector/database/mysql/mysql_client.py; affects QAnything before 1.2.0
CVE-2024-7099 (GHSA-vqmq-g46j-863w) CRITICAL CVSS 9.8 — SQL injection via unsanitized user input concatenated into queries in get_knowledge_base_name/delete_files; affects v1.4.1
CVE-2024-12866 (GHSA-6ch3-c5vc-j2r6) HIGH CVSS 7.5 — path traversal / local file inclusion allowing unauthenticated remote arbitrary file read; affects v2.0.0
CVE-2024-12864 (GHSA-w3m9-crxx-972f) HIGH — Denial of Service in the file-upload feature
CVE-2024-10264 (GHSA-jx9r-x782-9r4m) HIGH — HTTP request smuggling; affects v1.4.1
CVE-2024-8024 (GHSA-h35f-g2pq-8xq7) HIGH CVSS 7.5 — CORS misconfiguration; affects v1.4.1
CVE-2024-8026 (GHSA-m62f-m76v-gfwr) HIGH — CSRF in the backend API
auth_evidence: PRIMARY SOURCE: the quickstart (`git clone ... && cd QAnything && bash run.sh`) runs docker-compose-linux.yaml, which on master maps the app `"8777:8777"` and on qanything-v2 runs it under `network_mode: "host"` on 8777, with NO auth-related env var on the qanything_local service (only NCCL_LAUNCH_MODE, GPUID*, USER_IP/Gateway_IP). The official docs/API.md states the access URLs are `http://your_host:8777/qanything/` (frontend) and `http://your_host:8777/api/` with NO Authorization header, token, or API key on any endpoint; user identity is a free-text body parameter only: "if you don't need to distinguish different users, you can pass user_id='zzp'". This means a quickstart deployment exposed to the internet yields a fully unauthenticated UI AND REST API. Supporting backing-service tells in the same compose: elasticsearch `xpack.security.enabled=false`, mysql `MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=123456`, minio `MINIO_ACCESS_KEY=minioadmin / MINIO_SECRET_KEY=minioadmin`. In v2 (README): LLM model + OpenAI-compatible API key/base_url are "automatically saved in the front end settings" (configured via the unauth web UI, retrievable via get_bot_info), not via a protected env file.
sensitive_assets: Ingested RAG documents and their raw content / PII — list_files + get_file_base64 return original uploaded files (PDFs, contracts, internal docs) base64-encoded, fully readable unauth
Upstream LLM API keys and base_url — in v2 the OpenAI-compatible model API key is saved in front-end settings and retrievable via the unauth bot config (get_bot_info / new_bot)
Knowledge base definitions and business logic — list_knowledge_base / list_files enumerate every kb_id, kb_name, and document inventory by passing any user_id
Bot / agent definitions — new_bot/get_bot_info expose system prompts, model config, and bound knowledge bases (the business logic of the deployment)
Conversation / QA capability — local_doc_chat lets an unauth caller query the private corpus and exfiltrate answers (and run up the operator's LLM bill via the stored key)
Backing-store creds at defaults — minio minioadmin/minioadmin (object store of raw files), mysql root/123456, elasticsearch security disabled (vector/keyword index), Milvus standalone with no auth
sources: [CANDIDATE - NuClide Cat-34 rag-app-builders Stage -1 OSINT 2026-06-12; software-default characterization, no live host probed yet]
